conditions: The Climbing Claws are an accessory that lets players partially "stick" to the sides of blocks, slowly sliding down them when moving against them in midair. Jumps can also be performed off the sides of blocks. By repeatedly sticking and jumping, vertical blocks can be scaled. When sliding down, the slide speed can be accelerated using the ▼ Down key. Climbing Claws can be found in surface chests (including Water Chests ) and they also have a 1/100 (1%) chance of being found in Wooden Crates and Pearlwood Crates . They can also be purchased for 2 GC 50 SC from the Skeleton Merchant during a Waxing Crescent . Their availability very early in the game makes them a useful accessory for mobility, as they allow the player to slowly but surely climb up some walls without needing ropes .

The Cloud in a Bottle is an accessory that can be found in Gold Chests in the Underground and Cavern layers . When equipped, it allows the player an extra jump . The extra jump has a 75% jump duration compared to a normal jump, increasing the maximum height the player can reach without other accessories by 5 tiles, for a total of 11 tiles.

notes: It can be purchased from the Cyborg for 7 SC 50 CC each after completing the Martian Madness event.
Cluster Rocket IIs can be purchased from the Cyborg for 15 SC each during a Blood Moon or Solar Eclipse after the Martian Madness event has been defeated.

conditions: Cobwebs are crafting materials used to craft Silk and other items. Cobwebs can also be placed. Placed Cobwebs catch players who fall into them and reset fall damage . They break after a short time when a player is stuck in them, and they can also be harvested instantly with any weapon or swung tool .

Cobwebs are commonly found in underground areas in clusters of 5 or more alongside the edges of walls or ceilings, and are guaranteed to drop a Cobweb item when broken. They are also found in large quantities in Spider Nests , where they regrow over time; however, Cobwebs found there only have a 1/4 (25%) chance to drop when broken.

notes: The Code 1 is a pre-Hardmode yoyo that can be purchased from the Traveling Merchant for 5 GC once the Eye of Cthulhu has been defeated.
The Code 2 is a Hardmode , post one- mechanical boss yoyo that can be purchased from the Traveling Merchant for 25 GC .

Cogs are Hardmode crafting materials and placeable blocks that are purchased from the Steampunker for 7 SC each. They are used to make High Velocity Bullets , Liquid Sensors , and Steampunk furniture .

notes: The Companion Cube is a pet-summoning item that spawns a Companion Cube that follows the player. The Companion Cube can be purchased from the Traveling Merchant for 5 PC .
Confetti is a crafting material used to make several festive items. It is also used as ammunition for the Confetti Cannon . It is purchased from the Party Girl for 2 SC each.

The Confetti Cannon is a Hardmode novelty furniture / mechanism . It is purchased from the Party Girl for 25 GC , when the Pirate is present. Once placed, it can be fired by pressing ⚒ Use / Attack key on it with Confetti in hand, by pressing the Auto Select key with Confetti in the player's inventory, or tapping the Confetti Cannon with Confetti in hand.

The Confetti Gun is a consumable novelty weapon that fires a cloud of colorful confetti. This effect is purely visual and causes no damage or functional effect. Confetti Guns are consumed upon use; they can only be used once each. They are purchased from the Party Girl for 1 SC each.
The Copper Axe can also be purchased from the Merchant for 4 SC .

conditions: Copper Ore is a pre-Hardmode ore . It spawns in the Underground and Cavern layers , and occasionally on the surface . Copper Ore also has a small chance to appear as a bonus drop from slimes .

notes: The Copper Pickaxe is a pre-Hardmode pickaxe . It can mine blocks weaker than Meteorite . Newly created characters spawn with a Copper Pickaxe ( Iron Pickaxe in Journey Mode ). It can also be purchased from the Merchant for 5 SC .

conditions: Corals can be harvested either with a pickaxe / drill / explosives , by mining out the sand underneath them, or by placing a block above them. Afterwards, they can be placed on any type of block and will take on a random appearance out of six possible varieties.

notes: The Guide to Plant Fiber Cordage is an accessory that, when equipped, allows the player to collect Vine Ropes when destroying vines . The Guide to Plant Fiber Cordage has a 1/10 (10%) chance of being found inside surface Chests , or a 1/100 (1%) chance in Wooden Crates and Pearlwood Crates obtained from fishing . It can also be purchased from the Skeleton Merchant for 2 GC 50 SC during First Quarter moons.

The Guide to Plant Fiber Cordage has a 1/10 (10%) chance of being found inside surface Chests , or a 1/100 (1%) chance in Wooden Crates and Pearlwood Crates obtained from fishing . It can also be purchased from the Skeleton Merchant for 2 GC 50 SC during First Quarter moons.

conditions: The Corrupt Crate is a pre-Hardmode crate that can only be fished in The Corruption . It contains items found in standard crates and always contains an item normally dropped by destroying a Shadow Orb . Its Hardmode counterpart is the Defiled Crate .

The Defiled Crate is a Hardmode crate that can only be fished in The Corruption . Defiled Crates contain items found in standard crates, and additionally always drop an item normally obtained by destroying a Shadow Orb . Its pre-Hardmode counterpart is the Corrupt Crate .

The Corrupt Grass Wall is a type of background wall which can be found naturally generated in The Corruption , considered unsafe . It does not drop as an item when destroyed, and can only be obtained on Desktop , Console , and Mobile , by purchasing it from the Dryad during a Blood Moon for 2 SC 50 CC each. It is considered a safe wall when placed by the player.

notes: Corrupt Seeds are seeds used to plant Corrupt grass on Dirt Blocks , which will then spread The Corruption in all directions. They drop from the Eye of Cthulhu and can be purchased from the Dryad for 5 SC each during a Blood Moon in a world with Corruption. In a world with Crimson , Corrupt Seeds can be purchased from the Dryad if she is spoken to in a Hardmode Graveyard .

conditions: The Crimson Crate is a pre-Hardmode crate that can only be fished in The Crimson . It contains items found in standard crates and always contains an item normally dropped by destroying a Crimson Heart . Its Hardmode counterpart is the Hematic Crate .

The Hematic Crate is a Hardmode crate that can only be obtained by fishing in The Crimson . Hematic Crates contain items found in standard crates, and additionally always drop an item normally obtained by breaking a Crimson Heart . Its pre-Hardmode counterpart is the Crimson Crate .

The Crimson Grass Wall is a type of background wall which can be found naturally generated in The Crimson , considered unsafe . It does not drop as an item when destroyed, and can only be obtained on Desktop , Console , and Mobile , by purchasing it from the Dryad during a Blood Moon for 2 SC 50 CC each. It is considered a safe wall when placed by the player.

The Crimson Heart is a background object found deep in the chasms of the Crimson . A world generated with the Crimson features several Crimson Hearts, and destroying at least three of them (in a single world) is usually a crucial step in game advancement. This can be done using any hammer or explosives . Crimson Hearts release a fair amount of their own flickering light, similar to Crimson Altars . They are the Crimson counterpart to the Corruption 's Shadow Orbs .

The Crimson Rod has a 1/5 (20%) chance of being obtained from destroying Crimson Hearts found in the Crimson or a 1/5 (20%) chance from Crimson Crates and Hematic Crates , which are fished in the Crimson.

notes: Crimson Seeds are seeds used to plant Crimson grass on Dirt Blocks , which will then spread The Crimson in all directions. They drop from the Eye of Cthulhu and can be purchased from the Dryad for 5 SC each during a Blood Moon in a world with Crimson. In a world with Corruption , Crimson Seeds can be purchased from the Dryad if she is spoken to in a Hardmode Graveyard .

conditions: Crimson Tigerfish are a type of fish which are found commonly by fishing in the Crimson in any layer. They are used to craft Seafood Dinners and Heartreach Potions .
Crimstone is a block type found in The Crimson . Before Hardmode , it is most prominent in the lining of Chasms . It requires at least 65% Pickaxe power or explosives to mine.

The Crimstone Wall is a type of background wall which can be found naturally generated in The Crimson , considered unsafe . It does not drop as an item when destroyed, and can only be obtained on Desktop , Console , and Mobile , via crafting. It is considered a safe wall when placed by the player.

Crimtane Ore is a pre-Hardmode ore . It is primarily dropped from the Brain of Cthulhu , as well as the Eye of Cthulhu in Crimson worlds. Crimtane Ore can also be found rarely in small veins underground . When below 0 ft in elevation, a Gold Pickaxe or better is needed to mine it, otherwise any pickaxe can be used.

Crimtane Ore is a pre-Hardmode ore . It is primarily dropped from the Brain of Cthulhu , as well as the Eye of Cthulhu in Crimson worlds. Crimtane Ore can also be found rarely in small veins underground . When below 0 ft in elevation, a Gold Pickaxe or better is needed to mine it, otherwise any pickaxe can be used.

notes: Critter Shampoo is a vanity accessory equipped in a social accessory slot that changes the color of summoned minions when paired with a dye in the corresponding dye slot. It is purchased from the Zoologist for 10 GC after completing at least 30% (164 entries) of the Bestiary .

The Crystal Ball is a Hardmode furniture item which functions as a crafting station . It is purchased from the Wizard for 10 GC . Pressing ⚷ Open / Activate on a placed Crystal Ball grants the Clairvoyance buff and opens its crafting menu.

conditions: Unlike equipment buff items , the Crystal Ball must be placed in order to activate the buff. It can be mined with a pickaxe or drill and picked up again, even while the buff is active, which will not cancel the buff. Placement is only dependent on an adequate space of 2×2 tiles being available.

Crystal Shards are a common Hardmode crafting material found in the Underground Hallow . They can naturally grow on the left, right, and bottom sides of Pearlstone , Pink Ice , Pearlsand , Hardened Pearlsand , and Pearlsandstone Blocks . [ 1 ] Due to their constant regrowth, they can be farmed (see Guide:Farming resources § Crystal Shards for details) . Crystal Shards emit a small glow, similar to Demonite Ore and Crimtane Ore . They do not spread the Hallow .

Crystal Shards are also placeable in nearly any position (on walls, floors, and ceilings), though they are often not luminous enough to replace other light sources , and placement has no other functional effect. Although natural Crystal Shards can only grow on blocks related with the Hallow , they can be placed anywhere once harvested.

Cursed Flame is a Hardmode crafting material , whose Crimson counterpart is Ichor . It is dropped by World Feeders , Vile Ghouls (Desktop, Console and Mobile versions) and Clingers , all of which can be found in The Corruption . It can also be found in Defiled Crates (Desktop, Console and Mobile versions) .
